3A_Bibliographic.citation: Yang, A., Dong, G., Li, Y., Feng, J., Duan, J., Rolls, E. T., Zhang, X. M., & Cheng, W. (2022). Longer screen time utilization is associated with the polygenic risk for Attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder with mediation by brain white matter microstructure. eBioMedicine, 80, 104039.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: The participants for this analysis were from the ABCD cohort, an ongoing study of 11,000+ children who completed the baseline and follow-up assessments throughout the United States... Finally, 2,805,958 genetic variants and 4,673 samples remained for further analysis.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: 4673 European ancestry samples for PRS analyses; larger samples used for non-genetic analyses (e.g., 9459 for DTI at baseline). STU data available at baseline and 1-year; DTI at baseline and 2-year; ADHD scale at baseline, 1-year, and 2-year.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Screen time utilization (STU) - daily weighted average of self-reported time using electronic devices
3C_Exposure.modalities: total screen time (various electronic devices including Facebook, watching movies, etc.)
3C_Exposure.operationalization: Continuous (weighted sum: 5/7 x weekday hours + 2/7 x weekend hours)
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: Screen time utilization was assessed by the total amount of self-reported time using various electronic devices on both typical weekdays and weekend days (abcd_ssmty01), such as spending time on Facebook or watching movies. We calculated a weighted sum score to represent a daily STU as: 5/7 x hours of STU in a typical weekday + 2/7 x hours of STU in a typical weekend day.
3C_Exposure.notes: Note: In this paper STU is used both as exposure and outcome depending on the analysis. PRS_ADHD is the primary predictor and STU is the outcome in mediation analyses.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Screen time utilization (STU) as outcome of PRS_ADHD; also ADHD CBCL DSM5 Scale and white matter FA as intermediate outcomes
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: ABCD Youth Screen Time Survey (STU), Parent Report CBCL ADHD DSM5 Scale (cbcl_scr_dsm5_adhd), DTI fractional anisotropy (FA) from Atlas Track
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: To evaluate the children's level of ADHD symptoms, the ADHD CBCL DSM5 Scale from the Parent Report Child behavior checklist (CBCL, abcd_cbcls01) was used. The microstructural measures of fractional anisotropy (FA) ... were calculated by conventional DTI methods. The white matter fiber tracts were segmented using Atlas Track.
3D_Outcome.notes: Complex multimodal study. Primary focus is on the PRS_ADHD -> white matter FA -> ADHD scale -> STU pathway. Brain (white matter microstructure) is a key mediating outcome.

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Linear regression for cross-sectional associations, cross-lagged panel model (CLPM) for longitudinal analyses, mediation analysis (R Mediation package) and serial mediation (processR/PROCESS Macro model 6) with 10,000 bootstrap resampling iterations
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex, batch, total intracranial volume, first 10 genetic principal components, household income, parental education, site, body mass index, puberty
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: We fitted a linear regression model to investigate the relationship between the polygenic risk score for ADHD (PRS_ADHD), neural measures and behavioral assessments using R software (v3.6.3)... For multiple comparison purposes, false discovery rate (FDR) was adopted across all association analyses... A standard three-variable mediation analysis can be performed with the R package Mediation... We conducted a traditional cross-lagged panel model (CLPM) with the R package lavaan... a serial mediation analysis was performed by using model 6 in processR.
3E_Design_Analysis.notes: Count of 16 covariates includes the 10 genetic PCs counted as individual covariates.

3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: daily average screen time utilization (β = 0.18, se = 0.035, p = 2 × 10−7, t test, R2 = 1.0%)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome: Mean FA of all white matter tracts
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Standardized beta from linear regression; FA is fractional anisotropy
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: the mean FA value of all fibers tracts was found to be significantly correlated with PRS_ADHD (standardized β = -3.46, p = 5.48 × 10−4, t test, R2 = 0.33%, FDR < 0.05)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome: Mean FA of all white matter tracts
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Standardized beta; FA is fractional anisotropy
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: STU was significantly negatively correlated with the mean FA value of all tracts (standardized β = -3.23, p = 1.25 × 10−3, t test, R2 = 0.78%, FDR < 0.05)

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: Mean FA of white matter tracts (baseline)
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: the mean FA value of white matter tracts at baseline was significantly negativity correlated with the ADHD scale at 2-year follow-up (β = -0.039, se = 0.013, p = 2.94 × 10−3, z test, FDR < 0.05)
the ADHD scale at baseline was significantly correlated with STU at 1-year follow-up (β = 0.036, se = 0.009, p = 6.1 × 10−5, z test, FDR < 0.05)

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: PRS_ADHD -> FA white matter tracts (mediator) -> STU
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Indirect mediation effect (path AB), 3.17% of total effect
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: the FA value of white matter tracts... significantly mediated the relationship between PRS_ADHD and STU (path AB: accounting for 3.17% of the total effect, β = 0.006, 95% CI: [5.62 × 10−4, 0.012], p = 0.025, bootstrap test)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Indirect mediation effect (path AB), 14.78% of total effect
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: The ADHD scale was also a significant mediator between PRS_ADHD and STU (path AB: accounting for 14.78% of the total effect, β = 0.027, 95% CI: [0.016, 0.041], p < 1 × 10−4, bootstrap test)

3F_Effect_Sizes.predictor: PRS_ADHD -> FA white matter -> ADHD scale -> STU (serial mediation)
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome: STU (serial mediation through FA and ADHD scale)
3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Serial indirect mediation effect (path ADB), 0.53% of total effect
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: there was a significant indirect path between PRS_ADHD and STU through mean FA of white matter tracts and the ADHD scale (path ADB: accounting for 0.53% of the total effect, β = 0.001, 95% CI: [2.36 × 10−4, 1.93 × 10−3], p = 0.03, bootstrap test)

3G_Reporting_Quality.large_n_verbatim: the use of longitudinal data with a large number of participants leads to robust findings
3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: R2 values are very small (0.33%-1.0%), but the paper does not discuss the small magnitude of effects. CIs reported for mediation effects but not for all cross-sectional associations. Abstract does not use significance language or report p-values; it describes findings directionally.

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: These findings may be helpful when designing treatment strategies in future.
3H_Recommendations.notes: The paper makes a vague mention of treatment strategies but does not issue specific clinical or public health recommendations.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The discussion focuses on neural mechanisms linking ADHD polygenic risk to screen time through white matter microstructure. It interprets findings primarily in terms of biological plausibility (reward sensitivity, visual processing tracts, frontal-striatal circuitry) rather than emphasizing statistical significance or effect magnitude. The very small R2 values (0.33%-1.0%) are not discussed, and the paper frames the large sample as yielding 'robust findings' without acknowledging that small effects may reflect trivial associations.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: The standardized betas for DTI measures (e.g., -3.459) appear to be t-statistics rather than standardized regression coefficients, but the paper labels them 'std_beta'. This is unusual and creates ambiguity about effect magnitudes. The paper's primary focus is on genetic mediation pathways rather than a straightforward screen-time-to-outcome association.
